summary: Completed the MVP of the Vijdon Taxi Yo'lovchi (passenger) app end-to-end and confirmed it builds. Backend: added Client.user OneToOneField (migration 0043_client_user, nullable so bot-created clients stay valid), ClientRegisterSerializer, taxi/client_views.py + taxi/client_urls.py mounted at /client/ in config/urls.py — phone+password register/login/logout, home (map order-request form + live active-order tracking via polling), order create/poll/cancel (cancel only allowed pre-accept, mirroring the driver-side symmetric cancel-via-operator rule), history, profile (name edit + password change). Reused the existing dispatch_order/TariffSettings.calc_price/haversine/tg_new_order helpers so auto-dispatch and Telegram admin notifications work unchanged. Built taxi/templates/client/{base,login,register,home,history,profile,blocked}.html reusing the driver app's iOS-style design system and the same Yandex JS Maps click-to-pin pattern already used in the operator panel's order modal. Verified the whole flow with a Django test client against sqlite (register->login->home->order create->poll->cancel->history all returned expected responses). Could not run the real `migrate` — config/settings.py DATABASES points at a production Postgres host unreachable from this sandbox, so migration 0043 is generated but unapplied; needs `python manage.py migrate` on the actual server. Scaffolded vijdon_client_app (applicationId uz.vijdon.vijdon_client) as a Flutter WebView wrapper around https://vijdontaxi.uz/client/, modeled on vijdon_driver_app/lib/main.dart but stripped of driver-only pieces (foreground service, FCM/push, image picker, voice chat) per the user's explicit MVP scope decision (chat/SOS/push/rating deferred to a later phase). flutter analyze is clean and `flutter build apk --debug` succeeded (147MB debug APK at vijdon_client_app/build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-debug.apk) — confirms the whole project compiles.
current_status: Passenger MVP (register/login, map-based ride request, live status tracking, history, profile) is code-complete on both the Django backend and the new vijdon_client_app Flutter wrapper, and the Flutter build was verified locally. Remaining before this ships to real users: (1) apply migration 0043_client_user on the production Postgres database, (2) build/sign a release APK via vijdon_client_app/build_release.sh and distribute it, (3) manual on-device testing of the map/order flow against the live backend, (4) later phases explicitly deferred: in-app chat, SOS, push notifications for order updates, and passenger-rates-driver.
